outliers.source_caption:
You want to read this caption 

It was Saturday, July 15 a week or so after Fourth of July and I poured out the last little bit of alcohol. I had in my house and decided never again.

I had a large task in front of me. I had to build a six figure company in about 30 days to be able to afford to live out here and fight for my daughter’s custody.

I had everything stripped from me. I had shut down all my other business ventures to go all in on this business with my ex-wife.

And so when she put me on the street, I essentially had to whip up a six figure business in 30 days. There was no other option she had burned the boats. It wasn’t even my choice.

And faced with that, I had two choices I could lean into vices and slow slowly wait to die

Or I could become a man let go of boyish things remove the devices from my life and become everything she told me I never could be

And I did I built the business to six figures and then seven I want custody of my daughter and I can proudly say I’m the best dad I’ve ever met

I dealt with hardships, slander and living across the country with no family or friends to help me navigate life as a simple father and a business founder at the same time

I don’t know how I did it, but I can tell you two things. It wouldn’t have happened with alcohol and it wouldn’t have happened without Jesus Christ.

Here’s to 1000 days sober

Ave Ave Christus Rex

#soberliving #sobriety #fitnesslife #businessowner #1000days

outliers.recommended_angle: Translate the competitor's 'sobriety and custody' crisis into your 'burnout and imprisonment' crisis. Frame the decision to build an automated business not as a growth hack, but as a necessary act of survival to reclaim your life from the very thing you built.
my_hooks: This is a story about quitting.
The most important business decision I ever made was to disappear.

my_talking_points: It was a Tuesday afternoon in June. I was on a Zoom call I didn't need to be on, staring out the window at a perfect day, and I realized I had built myself a very comfortable prison.
I had a huge task in front of me. I had to rebuild my entire business in 90 days so it could run for a full month without me. Not just survive, but thrive.
I had followed the entire playbook. I’d scaled the revenue, hired the team, and done everything the gurus tell you to do.
But when I looked at my calendar, I saw that I had stripped away all my freedom. The business owned every hour of my day.
Faced with that, I had two choices. I could keep grinding, chase the next revenue milestone, and accept that this was just the price of success.
Or I could become a founder who actually had a life. I could let go of the need to be in every decision, remove myself as the bottleneck, and build something that truly served me.
So I did. I rebuilt the business around smart automation and a lean, empowered team. Then I disappeared for 30 days to go diving.
I came back, and the business had grown. It wouldn't have happened if I'd stayed addicted to the hustle.
The point isn't just to build online. It's to build online so you can live offline. That’s the entire game.
If you want the framework I used to automate my business and buy back my time, comment the word 'FieldWork' below. I'll send it to you.
